etymology.description: 馬路(馬路) is a combination of 馬(馬) (mǎ, "horse") and 路(路) (lù, "road"). In historical contexts, horses were a primary mode of transportation, thus 馬(馬) (mǎ, "horse") is used to indicate the traditional method of traveling on a 路(路) (lù, "road") or path.
etymology.interpretation: The term 馬路(馬路) (mǎlù, "road; street") reflects the historical importance of roads as pathways for horse-driven travel, now extending to all vehicles.

examples.chinese: 孩子们在马路上踢球(孩子們在馬路上踢球)。
examples.pinyin: Háizimen zài mǎlù shàng tī qiú.
examples.english: The children are playing ball on the road.

examples.chinese: 这条马路通向市中心(這條馬路通向市中心)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè tiáo mǎlù tōng xiàng shì zhōngxīn.
examples.english: This road leads to the city center.

examples.chinese: 马路两旁种满了树(馬路兩旁種滿了樹)。
examples.pinyin: Mǎlù liǎng páng zhòng mǎn le shù.
examples.english: Trees were planted on both sides of the street.
